title: Create Agenda package scaffolding and storage facade
goal: Establish the initial Agenda package structure and a stable public storage entrypoint so Stage 3 can grow behind one visible boundary.
context: Stage 3 begins the Agenda layer, which turns Formam deliverables into actionable work records and dependency structures.
Stage 2 already established canonical Formam storage and CLI routing, so Agenda should now begin with the same small-module discipline.
Wave A should focus on storage and record foundations only, not command behavior yet.
requirements: Create the azq/agenda package with import-safe scaffolding.
Add a storage.py module that serves as the initial public Agenda storage boundary.
Keep the facade minimal at first and avoid embedding full repository logic in this task.
Do not introduce CLI commands or workflow behavior in this task.
acceptance_criteria: azq.agenda imports cleanly.
azq.agenda.storage exists as the visible Stage 3 storage entrypoint.
The package scaffolding is ready for narrower storage modules to appear behind the facade.
suggested_commit_message: create agenda package scaffolding and storage facade

title: Add canonical Agenda path constants and directory helpers
goal: Define the canonical on-disk Agenda directories so task records, DAGs, and task logs live in predictable, inspectable locations.
context: Stage 1 and Stage 2 established the project pattern of explicit path modules and small directory helpers.
Agenda needs stable filesystem locations before task records and DAG artifacts can become durable evidence.
The implementation plan expects Agenda artifacts to be visible files rather than hidden internal state.
requirements: Define canonical path constants for Agenda data storage.
Include directory helpers for task records, DAG artifacts, and task log artifacts.
Keep path ownership centralized in a focused module instead of scattering raw path strings.
Expose path helpers through the Agenda storage boundary only where useful.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda path constants and directory helpers exist in a dedicated module.
Task, DAG, and log storage locations are explicit and inspectable.
The storage facade can rely on the path module without duplicating path logic.
suggested_commit_message: add canonical agenda path constants and directory helpers

title: Define the canonical normalized task-record schema
goal: Establish one canonical normalized schema for Agenda task records before any file-backed task repository is implemented.
context: Agenda should produce actionable work records without collapsing directly into vague activity tracking.
The Stage 3 build document describes canonical task records as visible durable artifacts.
Schema normalization should happen before repository reads and writes so later command flows do not drift.
requirements: Define one canonical normalized task-record schema in a dedicated schema module.
Include stable fields for task identity, parent deliverable linkage, title, status, and task intent metadata.
Normalize noisy or legacy-like input into the canonical schema where appropriate.
Keep task normalization distinct from DAG normalization.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda has one clear normalized task-record schema.
The schema is defined before task repository logic is introduced.
The storage facade can expose the normalization boundary without embedding all schema details inline.
suggested_commit_message: define canonical normalized agenda task schema

title: Implement markdown task-record serialization, parsing, and repository reads
goal: Create the first file-backed Agenda repository for one-task-per-file records using a human-readable Markdown format.
context: Stage 1 and Stage 2 used Markdown as the primary durable artifact format for canonical records.
Agenda task records should be inspectable, diff-friendly, and readable by a human operator at the terminal.
Stage 3 should not hide task state inside opaque blobs when visible files can carry the truth.
requirements: Implement a one-task-per-file repository for canonical Agenda task records.
Use a human-readable Markdown format with stable metadata and readable body content.
Keep the on-disk format diff-friendly and avoid hiding the primary record inside an opaque JSON blob unless absolutely necessary.
Add parser and serializer helpers for the canonical task-record format.
Add repository read helpers and next-id generation for task files.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda can serialize and parse canonical task records as Markdown.
One task is stored per file in a predictable canonical location.
Repository reads and next-id generation operate over canonical task files.
The task-record format is human-readable and inspectable from the filesystem.
suggested_commit_message: implement markdown agenda task records and repository reads

title: Add canonical deliverable-parent validation for Agenda tasks
goal: Require every Agenda task record to point back to a valid canonical Formam deliverable through the Formam storage boundary.
context: Stage 2 made Formam the authoritative layer for deliverables and goal maps.
Agenda must remain downstream of Formam rather than inventing a parallel interpretation of deliverables.
The implementation plan expects tasks to descend from deliverables, not float free of them.
requirements: Add validation that every Agenda task points to a canonical parent deliverable.
Perform deliverable-parent validation through the Formam storage boundary rather than duplicating deliverable semantics inside Agenda.
Reject or clearly fail task records that reference missing deliverables.
Keep this task focused on validation helpers, not command behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda task validation confirms that the referenced deliverable exists canonically in Formam.
Agenda does not create a second storage authority for deliverable truth.
Invalid parent-deliverable references fail clearly.
suggested_commit_message: add canonical deliverable parent validation for agenda tasks

title: Define the canonical normalized task-DAG schema
goal: Establish one canonical normalized schema for Agenda dependency graphs before DAG artifacts are written to disk.
context: Agenda needs explicit dependency structures so work order is visible and not implied by file order or wishful thinking.
The Stage 3 plan distinguishes between task records and DAG artifacts, so their schemas should also remain distinct.
A canonical DAG schema makes later command and validation work less brittle.
requirements: Define one canonical normalized DAG schema in the Agenda schema module.
Keep DAG normalization separate from task-record normalization.
Include stable graph-level identity and edge representation fields.
Keep the schema readable and easy to validate.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda has one clear normalized DAG schema.
Task-record and DAG schemas are distinct and not muddled together.
The storage facade can re-export DAG normalization without owning the underlying schema logic.
suggested_commit_message: define canonical normalized agenda dag schema

title: Implement canonical DAG artifact serialization and repository helpers
goal: Create the first file-backed Agenda DAG repository so dependency maps become durable visible artifacts.
context: Agenda needs durable dependency artifacts to support later planning and execution commands.
The Stage 3 build note suggests DAGs can be stored in a structured format distinct from Markdown task records.
The Stage 2 pattern favors narrow repository modules rather than one giant storage file.
requirements: Implement canonical DAG serialization and parsing helpers.
Add repository helpers for reading and writing DAG artifacts.
Keep DAG artifact storage explicit and inspectable on disk.
Keep DAG storage logic in a dedicated module rather than pushing it into the task-record repository.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda can read and write canonical DAG artifacts through dedicated helpers.
DAG storage logic lives in a focused module.
Dependency artifacts are durable visible files in canonical locations.
suggested_commit_message: implement canonical agenda dag storage helpers

title: Implement canonical task-log storage helpers
goal: Create visible per-task log artifact helpers so execution notes and task-local history have a stable place to live.
context: The Stage 3 build draft expects task logs to exist as visible artifacts rather than disappearing into ephemeral output.
Log artifacts should be task-local evidence, not a replacement for canonical task records or DAG truth.
Wave A should create the storage foundation, not full execution workflows.
requirements: Implement canonical helpers for per-task log artifact paths and writes.
Keep log storage separate from task-record and DAG storage.
Make log artifacts visible and inspectable on disk.
Do not broaden this task into execution orchestration.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda has dedicated helpers for canonical task-log artifact storage.
Task logs have stable visible locations.
Log storage remains a supporting artifact layer rather than the primary task truth.
suggested_commit_message: implement canonical agenda task log storage helpers

title: Reduce azq.agenda.storage to a thin public facade
goal: Finish the Wave A foundation by making azq/agenda/storage.py a stable thin public boundary over the focused Agenda storage modules.
context: Stage 2 ended with a refactor wave that preferred narrow modules behind a stable visible facade.
Wave A should close with a public Agenda storage boundary that later waves can build commands against safely.
This is a closeout step for the Stage 3 foundation, not a place to add new behavior.
requirements: Make azq.agenda.storage the stable public facade for the Wave A storage surface.
Let the facade re-export the public helpers that later waves should rely on.
Do not let the facade own repository implementation logic, serialization details, or id-allocation internals directly.
Keep the facade import-safe and readable.
acceptance_criteria: azq.agenda.storage re-exports the intended public Agenda storage helpers.
The facade owns no primary serialization logic.
The facade owns no primary id-allocation logic.
The facade owns no repository implementation logic.
Later waves can depend on azq.agenda.storage as the stable public boundary.
suggested_commit_message: reduce agenda storage to a thin public facade
